diagrams.title: Linear Search vs Binary Search Comparison
diagrams.description: A visual comparison showing how Linear Search checks each element sequentially while Binary Search halves the search space each iteration
elements: Binary search path: 7→3→1→0 (target at index 0, worst case iterations: 4)
Time complexity annotations: O(n) vs O(log n)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the exponential advantage of binary search over linear search for large datasets

diagrams.title: Array Insertion Operation
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step process of inserting element X at position 2 in an array of size 5
elements: Initial array state with elements A,B,C,D,E
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why array insertion has O(n) complexity — shifting requires moving each element

diagrams.title: Linked List Insertion at Head
diagrams.description: Demonstrates O(1) insertion at the beginning of a singly linked list
elements: Create new node with data X
diagrams.purpose: Compare with array insertion to show why linked lists are more efficient for front insertions
